patch.value: Found during audit #12 external CJS-host verification (note on pm-rjqr): raw client.run('create', {type,title}) fails with 'Missing required option --title' because run() expects pre-split argv-style args, while the typed client.create() works. Agents composing SDK calls dynamically will hit this. Either map structured payload keys onto flags inside run(), or document the contract and emit a helpful error suggesting the typed primitive.
SDK client.run() rejects structured payloads for create: raw {type,title} fails with 'Missing required option --title'

client.run('create', ...) either accepts bare title/type keys by mapping them to CLI flags, or the split-args contract is documented in docs/SDK.md with a typed error pointing callers to client.create(); regression test covers both paths

author: codex-agent

patch.value: SDK client.run currently rejects structured create payloads such as raw {type,title}; the fix should accept typed structured inputs or return a precise SDK-facing error instead of CLI flag parsing text.
